highlights.text: I wrote on Wednesday at 3pm: “Bear case tomorrow/today: Begins under 6982… Perhaps 6979 trigger.” We hit that 6979 sell trigger early Thursday morning, and down we went to 6900 elevator down .
The most common of these is what I call the two siblings: The first sibling is ES suddenly goes elevator down .
This always follows the elevator down sell - never any exception in recorded history.
However, the short squeeze only occurs when ES puts in my core setup: The Failed Breakdown.

Institutions buy dips by waiting for a news driven elevator down flush/trap , waiting for a low to be taken out in the process, trapping shorts beneath the low, using that liquidity to fund their longs, and then price rips when the low recovers.
This would mean a Failed Breakdown and short squeeze was coming From there, institutions stepped in as always.
I wrote also Wednesday at 3pm: “There is some support at 6923 but instead of buying this wait for the Failed Breakdown of 6923.

We set a huge low Monday morning at 4:45AM at 6923 and the flush and recovery of it is actionable.” By 11:20AM, we bounced at ~6900, recovered 6923, the Failed Breakdown was triggered, and we ripped to 6990+ yesterday.

I wrote yesterday at 4pm: “A possible entry is if ES can flush 6935 (ideally down to 6925) then recover 6935, and this is a clear Failed Breakdown.” We saw exactly this around 1pm today: We flushed 6935 down to 6920ish, recovered 6935, and ripped.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow.

post_title: SPX Is Yet Again Coiled Tight. Break Coming. What Way? Feb 2nd Plan
